content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Amendment fr-16-c308d06c identifies a confirmed bug in `capture_next_step_hint`. When `bv --robot-next` returns a success response like `{"message":"No actionable items available"}`, `exec_json::<NextBeadResponse>` fails deserialization (since `NextBeadResponse` requires `id` and `title` fields), producing a `BvParseError` that lands in the `BvFailed` arm. The `BvFailed` arm intentionally preserves existing hints (correct for transient errors), but this means a stale `next_step_hint.json` pointing to an already-completed bead survives after the final actionable bead closes. The fix—distinguishing a successful 'no items' response from a true failure and clearing the persisted hint—is correct and well-scoped.

## Votes

- `fr-16-c308d06c`: Accept
  Verified the bug in the source code. `capture_next_step_hint` (line 361-373) calls `bv.exec_json::<NextBeadResponse>()` which requires `id` and `title` fields in the JSON. A successful `{"message":"No actionable items available"}` response will fail `serde_json::from_str` at `bv_process.rs:426`, producing `BvParseError`, which maps to `HintCaptureOutcome::BvFailed` at line 370. The `BvFailed` arm at lines 177-181 deliberately leaves existing hints untouched—correct for transient errors but wrong for a successful 'no more work' signal. This will manifest every time the last actionable bead in a milestone completes, leaving a stale `next_step_hint.json` that could misdirect downstream selection logic. The fix is straightforward: either add a new `HintCaptureOutcome` variant (e.g., `NoActionableItems`) or attempt to parse the message-only shape before `NextBeadResponse`, then clear/remove the persisted hint file. The regression test request is appropriate and will guard against future regressions in this parsing path.
